purpose: Phase-1 investigation findings prior to plan authoring. Documents architectural facts that materially refine the original handoff framing of sanitizer-removal-v1 (work-dag-2026-04-28 §10).

claim_1_detect_sanitizer_ast_dead_code.handoff_claim: detect_sanitizer_ast is dead code — exists at taint.rs:3333, exported in mod.rs:22, ZERO call sites in compute_taint_with_tree or compute_taint.
claim_1_detect_sanitizer_ast_dead_code.verification: VERIFIED with line-number correction. detect_sanitizer_ast is at taint.rs:3490 (post-Wave-2 + post-field_access_info-extension-v1 the file grew). Exported at mod.rs:22. Searched the entire crates/ tree for callers: zero matches outside the definition site. Only detect_sanitizer (the regex variant at taint.rs:1096) is invoked, at exactly two call sites: taint.rs:4101 (inside process_block, M1a String-keyed worklist branch) and taint.rs:4350 (inside ssa_propagate, M1b SSA-versioned branch). Both call sites are inside the per-line dispatch in compute_taint_with_tree.

claim_2_zero_content_banks.handoff_claim: 5 zero-content AST sanitizer banks: C×3, LUA_AST_SANITIZERS, OCAML_AST_SANITIZERS
claim_2_zero_content_banks.verification: FALSIFIED. ALL 16 AST sanitizer banks are non-empty at HEAD e69eed3. Per-bank entry counts (verified by reading the file directly):

RUST_AST_SANITIZERS.summary: Numeric — single AstSanitizerPattern with member_patterns covering 8 turbofish forms (.parse::<i32> through .parse::<isize>)

C_AST_SANITIZERS.summary: Numeric (atoi/atol/atof/strtol/strtoul/strtod via call_names), Shell (snprintf via call_names)

claim_2_zero_content_banks.reframe_implication: The handoff framing said 'parity-fill 5 zero-content banks'. That is incorrect: there are NO zero-content banks at HEAD e69eed3. Either (a) the handoff was written against an older HEAD where some AST sanitizer banks did not yet exist (e.g., pre-W1.5 where regex-removal-v1 W1-M5 did some sanitizer parity work), and the gap was closed during regex-removal-v1; or (b) the 'zero-content' claim was a misreading of regex-removal-v1 worker-2-integ-tests.json:530 which actually says '5 banks have ZERO member_patterns and 100% call_names' — i.e., 5 banks (Rust, Kotlin, C×Numeric+Shell, Lua, OCaml) cover their patterns via call_names with zero member_patterns. That is NOT the same as 'zero-content'. Both interpretations are reconcilable with the present-day non-empty state.
claim_2_zero_content_banks.scope_implication: M3 in the original framing (parity-fill 5 zero-content banks) is REPLACED by a smaller scope: a parity-AUDIT (not parity-fill) that verifies each of the 16 regex-bank entries has a corresponding AST entry. If the audit surfaces any genuine gap (regex catches X; AST does not), that becomes a parity-add for that specific entry. Initial scan suggests this is mostly closed already — see claim_3 below.

claim_3_regex_to_ast_parity.approach: Compare each language's regex sanitizer Vec entries against its AST_SANITIZERS bank. Look for regex patterns that match shapes the AST cannot.

Python.ast_coverage: PYTHON_AST_SANITIZERS has all 3: call_names: [int, float, bool] Numeric; member_patterns: [(shlex,quote), (pipes,quote)] Shell; member_patterns: [(html,escape), (markupsafe,escape), (cgi,escape)] Html. PARITY: full

TypeScript_JavaScript.ast_coverage: TYPESCRIPT_AST_SANITIZERS — verify by reading L1985-2061. Regex .(parse|safeParse) is wildcard-receiver shape (any object's .parse or .safeParse method). AST equivalent would be ('*', 'parse'), ('*', 'safeParse').

Go.ast_coverage: GO_AST_SANITIZERS — verify member_patterns shape for strconv.{Atoi,ParseInt,ParseFloat} + html.EscapeString + url.QueryEscape

Rust.ast_coverage: RUST_AST_SANITIZERS uses raw-substring fallback shape ('', '.parse::<i32>') etc — 8 entries. Tree-sitter-rust parses turbofish as a generic_function call, not a field_expression — raw fallback is the only viable shape. PARITY: full but via raw-substring
Rust.gap: none — raw-substring fallback is the canonical AST shape for turbofish

Cpp.ast_coverage: CPP_AST_SANITIZERS uses raw-substring for std::sto* (qualified_identifier, not field_expression) and for static_cast<T> (template-call shape). Both are raw-substring. PARITY: full via raw-substring

Ruby.ast_coverage: RUBY_AST_SANITIZERS — verify shape. .to_i is a method-call on any receiver — likely ('*', 'to_i'). Rack::Utils uses :: scope_resolution (per regex-removal-v1 W3 Ruby risk register), which extract_call_name_ruby may NOT cover. CGI.escapeHTML is normal Module.function shape.
Ruby.gap: FLAG — Rack::Utils.escape_html scope_resolution shape may be uncovered by AST. M1 must verify.

Kotlin.ast_coverage: KOTLIN_AST_SANITIZERS = single AstSanitizerPattern with member_patterns: [('*', 'toInt'), ('*', 'toLong'), ('*', 'toDouble'), ('*', 'toFloat')] — wildcard receiver. PARITY: full

Swift.ast_coverage: SWIFT_AST_SANITIZERS — verify Int/Double/Float as call_names + addingPercentEncoding shape

CSharp.ast_coverage: CSHARP_AST_SANITIZERS — verify member_patterns (int.Parse uses primitive-type-name receiver; Convert.ToInt32 is normal Module.fn; HttpUtility.HtmlEncode is normal Module.fn)

Scala.ast_coverage: SCALA_AST_SANITIZERS — verify shape (.toInt is method call on any receiver — wildcard)

PHP.ast_coverage: PHP_AST_SANITIZERS — verify shape. NOTE: The regex covers TWO shapes: (a) call_expression for intval()/floatval()/htmlspecialchars()/htmlentities()/mysqli_real_escape_string(); (b) cast_expression for (int)/(float). Cast_expression is NOT a call_expression in tree-sitter-php — likely needs raw-substring fallback for the cast forms.
PHP.gap: FLAG — (int)/(float) PHP cast_expression shape. M1 must verify and add raw-substring fallback if missing.

Lua.ast_coverage: LUA_AST_SANITIZERS = single AstSanitizerPattern with call_names: ['tonumber']. PARITY: full

Elixir.ast_coverage: ELIXIR_AST_SANITIZERS — verify member_patterns. String.to_integer/to_float is normal Module.fn; Phoenix.HTML.html_escape is multi-segment receiver — same shape as Ecto.Adapters.SQL.query already verified in field_access_info-extension-v1.

OCaml.ast_coverage: OCAML_AST_SANITIZERS = single AstSanitizerPattern with call_names: ['int_of_string', 'float_of_string']. PARITY: full

claim_4_dispatch_signature_compatibility.investigation: Wiring detect_sanitizer_ast into the per-line dispatch loop requires propagating tree + source through the call chain.

current_signatures.process_block: fn process_block(block_id: usize, mut current_taint: HashSet<String>, refs_by_block: &HashMap<usize, Vec<&VarRef>>, statements: &HashMap<u32, String>, _line_to_block: &HashMap<u32, usize>, sources_by_line: &HashMap<u32, HashSet<String>>, sanitized_vars: &mut HashSet<String>, language: Language) -> HashSet<String>  -- declared at taint.rs:4061
current_signatures.ssa_propagate_ctx: struct SsaPropagateCtx<'a> { ssa, sources, predecessors, successors, line_to_block, statements, language, max_iterations }  -- declared at taint.rs:4174
current_signatures.compute_taint_with_tree: Has tree: Option<&tree_sitter::Tree>, source: Option<&[u8]> already at L3543-3544

claim_4_dispatch_signature_compatibility.implication: Both process_block and SsaPropagateCtx need an EXTENSION to carry Optional<&Tree> + Option<&[u8]>. This is an internal API change (private fn signature + private struct field), NO public API change. The wiring at L4101 / L4350 calls detect_sanitizer (regex) with `stmt` string; the AST equivalent calls detect_sanitizer_ast(root, source, language, line) which needs (a) the tree's root_node, (b) the source bytes, (c) the line. The dispatch must construct a `(line, stmt)` tuple and consult AST first, fall back to regex (paralleling the source/sink AST-with-regex-fallback pattern already established in compute_taint_with_tree at L3565-L3606).
claim_4_dispatch_signature_compatibility.wiring_strategy: AST-FIRST-WITH-REGEX-FALLBACK during M2 (additive). When AST fires, mark sanitized; otherwise fall through to regex. This preserves all current sanitizer behaviour while enabling the AST path. After M3 parity-audit gates green for all 16 langs, M4 atomically deletes the regex banks AND switches the dispatch from AST-with-fallback to AST-only.

claim_5_taintinfo_extension.question: Does TaintInfo / TaintFlow already have a sanitization concept, or do we need to extend the API?
claim_5_taintinfo_extension.verification: VERIFIED. TaintInfo already has sanitized_vars (HashSet<String>) populated by both regex (process_block at L4102) and SSA (ssa_propagate at L4352) paths. The wiring at the new AST call site simply also writes into sanitized_vars. NO TaintInfo / TaintFlow API extension required.

claim_6_compute_taint_refactor.question: Does compute_taint (the legacy non-tree entry) need a refactor analogous to regex-removal-v1 W2-M11?
claim_6_compute_taint_refactor.verification: NO. compute_taint at L3993 is already an internal-parse-and-delegate stub that reconstructs source from the line-keyed statements map, parses via tldr_core::ast::parser::parse, and delegates to compute_taint_with_tree. This was done in regex-removal-v1 W2-M11 atomic. So all sanitizer dispatch flows through compute_taint_with_tree regardless of entry point — wiring in compute_taint_with_tree is sufficient.

claim_7_obsolete_unit_tests_count.question: How many existing taint_tests.rs tests will become obsolete when regex sanitizer banks are deleted in M4?
claim_7_obsolete_unit_tests_count.investigation: Searched taint_tests.rs for tests calling detect_sanitizer / find_sanitizers_in_statement / patterns.sanitizers iteration. Found: lines 330, 342, 354, 976, 981, 986, 991, 1003, 1008, 1020, 1025, 1030, 1035, 1047, 1052, 1064, 1069, 1081, 1086 — these are SanitizerType assertions inside larger test bodies. Need a full enumeration of test fn bounds (NOT just line numbers) before M1 starts. Predecessor field_access_info-extension-v1 plan deferred this enumeration to M1 (executor-level); same approach here.
claim_7_obsolete_unit_tests_count.preliminary_estimate: There are at least 3 test functions explicitly devoted to detect_sanitizer assertions (Ruby L1253, Elixir L1518, OCaml L1638 — 'KEEP' list per field_access_info dispatch contract). PLUS test_python_detect_sanitizers / test_typescript / test_go / test_java / test_rust / test_c / test_cpp / test_kotlin / test_swift / test_csharp / test_scala / test_php / test_lua tests at the L320-L1100 region (via detect_sanitizer or find_sanitizers_in_statement). Final count must be enumerated by executor in M1.
claim_7_obsolete_unit_tests_count.preliminary_count_range: 13 to 16 test functions (one per language matching the pattern test_<lang>_detect_sanitizers)

scope_revisions_from_handoff.from_handoff: 1. Wire detect_sanitizer_ast | 2. Parity-fill 5 zero-content AST sanitizer banks (C×3 + Lua + OCaml) | 3. Delete 16 regex sanitizer banks
scope_revisions_from_handoff.revised_per_investigation: 1. Wire detect_sanitizer_ast (M2) — extends process_block + SsaPropagateCtx signatures with Option<&Tree> + Option<&[u8]>. AST-first-with-regex-fallback during transition. | 2. Parity-AUDIT 16 AST sanitizer banks (M3) — all banks non-empty; audit verifies each regex entry has an AST equivalent. Two FLAG candidates (Ruby Rack::Utils scope_resolution, PHP cast_expression) need verification + likely raw-substring fallback addition. | 3. Atomic delete (M4) — 16 regex sanitizer banks + obsolete unit tests + dispatch flip from AST-with-fallback to AST-only.
scope_revisions_from_handoff.loc_delta_revision: Original implied massive parity-fill work (~+200 LOC). Investigation suggests the parity-add work is small (likely ~+10-20 LOC for the 2 flagged entries). Net milestone is dominated by regex-bank deletion (~ -120 to -150 LOC for 16 sanitizer Vecs + ~13-16 unit tests at ~30 LOC avg = -390 to -480 LOC for tests; total net negative ~ -300 to -400 LOC + 80-100 LOC of integration tests = net ~ -250 LOC.

blocking_or_escalation: NONE. All architectural facts are reconcilable; milestone scope is well-defined and executable. Plan and dispatch contract authored on 2026-04-29.
next_steps: Author plan.md mirroring field_access_info-extension-v1 §0 critical-reframe pattern
Author dispatch-contract.json with milestones M1 (RED tests + AST-only harness) → M2 (wire) → M3 (parity-audit + add 2 flagged) → M4 (atomic delete) → M5 (CHANGELOG + tag)

Validator/premortem pass not strictly required — single-worker investigation matches field_access_info-extension-v1 cadence; carry-forward exception list and validator_mandates baked into the dispatch contract
